title: Extend geometric sequences
description: Given the first few terms in a geometric sequence, find the next term in the sequence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the next term of the geometric sequence?**

 $2, 6, 18,$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
Each term is $\maroonD{3}$ times the term before it.

| $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times 3\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times 3\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | 
:-: |  :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | 
$2,$ | | $6,$ | | $18$
The next term is $18\cdot\maroonD{3}=54$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the next term of the geometric sequence?**

 $3, 6, 12,$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
Each term is $\maroonD{2}$ times the term before it.

| $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times 2\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times 2\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | 
:-: |  :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | 
$3,$ | | $6,$ | | $12$
The next term is $12\cdot\maroonD{2}=24$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the next term of the geometric sequence?**

 $250, 50, 10,$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
Each term is a fifth of the term before it. In other words, each term is $\maroonD{\dfrac15}$ times the term before it.

| $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times \dfrac15\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | $\footnotesize\maroonC{\times \dfrac15\,\Large\curvearrowright}$ | | 
:-: |  :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | 
$250,$ | | $50,$ | | $10$
The next term is $10\cdot\maroonD{\dfrac15}=2$.

:-: |  :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: |
